NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION OF PLAINTIFF TO AMEND FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT; PROPOSED SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ATTACHED AS EXHIBIT 1 (TRANSACTION ID # 57760488) FILED BY PLAINTIFF ESPARZA, NINFA HEARING SET FOR OCT-05-2015 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-05-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NINFA ESPARZA'S MOTION TO AMEND FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT MAKES DISCLOSURE. NO OBJECTIONS MADE BY COUNSEL TO THE COURT HEARING THIS MATTER. MATTER ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY COUNSEL. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT WHICH ALLEGES THE LOCATIONS OF THE PARTIES AND CLAIMS FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AND VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY BASED ON THE ALLEGED DELAY IN RECEIPT OF SDI BENEFITS, BUT IS NOT PERMITTED TO ALLEGE A CLAIM FOR FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE A DISABILITY BASED ON THE ALLEGED DELAY IN RECEIPT OF SDI BENEFITS. IN THE EARLY STAGES OF A CASE, AS HERE, LEAVE TO AMEND SHOULD BE DENIED BASED ON THE LEGAL INSUFFICIENCY OF THE MATTERS SOUGHT TO BE AMENDED ONLY WHEN THERE IS NO REASONABLE ARGUMENT ABOUT THE LEGAL INSUFFICIENCY OF THE MATTERS SOUGHT TO BE AMENDED. AS APPLIED HERE, WHILE IT IS CLEAR THAT THERE IS NO POSSIBLE COGNIZABLE CLAIM FOR FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE BASED ON THE ALLEGED DELAY IN RECEIPT OF SDI, THERE IS NO SUCH CLARITY AS TO THE CLAIMS FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AND VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY BASED ON THE ALLEGED DELAY IN RECEIPT OF SDI BENEFITS. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Attorney Bruce Highman telephone number 415-982-5563 for Plaintiff Ninfa Esparza.
Elizabeth O'Brien of Gordon & Rees LLP telephone number 915-565-2900 for Defendant FCI Federal, Inc.

Judge Kahn made a disclosure regarding his relationship with Mr. Highman and stated that it does not affect his ability to be fair and impartial. The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court changed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FCI FEDERAL, INC'S DEMURRER TO the fourth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty in the second amended complaint is overruled. Mr. Highman is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Attorney Bruce Highman telephone number 415-982-5563 for Plaintiff Ninfa Esparza.
Elizabeth O'Brien of Gordon & Rees LLP telephone number 915-565-2900 for Defendant FCI Federal, Inc.

Judge Kahn made a disclosure regarding his relationship with Mr. Highman and stated that it does not affect his ability to be fair and impartial. The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court changed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FCI FEDERAL, INC'S motion To Strike the punitive damages allegations from the second amended complaint is denied as to the first three claims and granted as to the breach of fiduciary duty claim. Mr. Highman is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-09-2015 Continued To Jan-20-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff To Amend First Amended Complaint; Proposed Second Amended Complaint Attached As Exhibit 1
Off Calendar Oct-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. BRUCE J. HIGHMAN, ESQ. of Highman & Highman, Tel No. 415-982-5563 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. ELIZABETH O'BRIEN, ESQ. of Gordon & Rees, LLP, Tel No. 916-565-2900 appearing telephonically for Defendant FCI Federal, Inc.

RULING - The Court makes disclosure regarding personal relationship with Plaintiff's counsel. No objections made by counsel to the Court hearing this matter. Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF NINFA ESPARZA'S Motion To Amend First Amended Complaint is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff is granted leave to file a second amended complaint which alleges the locations of the parties and claims for breach of fiduciary duty and violation of public policy based on the alleged delay in receipt of SDI benefits, but is not permitted to allege a claim for failure to accommodate a disability based on the alleged delay in receipt of SDI benefits. In the early stages of a case, as here, leave to amend should be denied based on the legal insufficiency of the matters sought to be amended only when there is no reasonable argument about the legal insufficiency of the matters sought to be amended. As applied here, while it is clear that there is no possible cognizable claim for failure to accommodate based on the alleged delay in receipt of SDI, there is no such clarity as to the claims for breach of fiduciary duty and violation of public policy based on the alleged delay in receipt of SDI benefits. Plaintiff's counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)
